cards.back: A combination of physical RAM and hard disk storage that appears as a single, contiguous block of memory to the operating system
Memory that is moved from physical RAM to hard disk storage temporarily, until it is needed again
An exception that occurs when the operating system tries to access a memory page that is not in physical RAM
The process of moving pages of memory from physical RAM to hard disk storage to free up memory

cards.front: What is the purpose of Virtual Memory Management?
cards.back: To allow a program to use more memory than is physically available in RAM, while also preventing memory exhaustion and improving system performance
